what_it_does: Concurrent Cognition engine proving spike: file-discovered registries for roles, skills, contexts, guides, decisions, and generation policies; a role runner supporting generate/embed ops, addressed run:// resolution, a generation-policy repetition-penalty ladder, staged two-part turns, concurrent waves, jury/panel/reduce/cascade/chunked helpers, and VRAM slot budgeting.
observations: Roles, skills, contexts, guides, decisions, and generation policies are discovered fresh from filesystem directories on each call.
run_role supports both generate and embed ops, with input and op axes that stay byte-identical for existing utterance-only callers.
A generation-policy ladder escalates repetition_penalty when finish_reason is length and fails loud when the ladder is exhausted.
run:// addresses are resolved via store.head and store.get_content, not in-memory thread returns.
The recall injection rule is a declared AST evaluated by runtime/rules.py rather than hand-written Python.
chat_parts_spike demonstrates a two-part staged turn with recall and ground firing concurrently.
embed_corpus_to_spaces delegates persistence to the existing vector_index.build_index path instead of creating a second vector pipeline.
Multiple orchestration primitives live here: run_swarm, run_items, run_chunked, run_jury, run_panel, run_reduce, and run_cascade.
Fail-loud semantics are emphasized: missing inputs, unresolved addresses, and down models raise rather than silently degrade.
The file is heavily commented and self-describes its evolution through G0, G2, G3, O2, O3, and C generalizations.
standards: run_role's dual op axis and optional generation-policy ladder.
File-discovered registries as the single source of truth for roles, skills, contexts, guides, decisions, and policies.
The injection_rule as a declared AST routed through runtime/rules.py.
chat_parts_spike as the explicit two-part staged-turn proof.
embed_corpus_to_spaces bridging corpus capture into queryable projection spaces.
conventions: Registry-is-truth: runtime data is loaded from files rather than hard-coded.
Additive, behavior-preserving changes keep existing callers byte-identical.
Fail-loud: missing values, unresolved addresses, and model failures raise.
run:// addressing throughout; no swarm:// scheme.
Lazy imports for decoupled modules like runtime.decision_registry and runtime.decision_subtypes.
Dataclasses carry execution artifacts (StagedTurn, RoleRun, WaveResult, ItemsResult, etc.).
concerns: The file mixes many responsibilities: registries, runner, resolver, orchestration, budgeting, and spike demo.
Very long docstrings and inline commentary may impede scanning and maintenance.
Module-level registry discovery and spike role lookups happen at import time, which can fail loudly if role files are missing.
It manipulates sys.path to import ops/cli/capabilities, coupling runtime to CLI layout.
Nested helper functions reuse names like _one and _resolve_unit across different parents, which can shadow or confuse.
The generation-policy ladder is implemented but the transport does not yet forward repetition_penalty, per the file's own flag.
The file is truncated in the provided view, so the full implementations of run_cascade and later helpers are not visible.
notes: A heavily documented proving spike that grew into a broad runtime orchestration layer, preserving existing callers while adding registry-driven concurrency primitives.
questions: Where exactly are runtime.roles, runtime.rules, and runtime.resolver defined and what are their contracts?
What is the exact interface of the store object used for head, get_content, put_content, and set_ref?
How does the Role dataclass represent input_addresses, prompt_slot, schema_slot, thinking, and op?
How do generation_policies files map ids to ladder data?
What is the intended relationship between run_swarm and the existing scheduler.py?
Does the transport layer plan to pass repetition_penalty, and who owns that change?
What is _SERVICES_JSON and where is it maintained?
How are decision_registry rows and decision_take marks composed into resolved state?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring frames it as a minimal G0 spike and then documents successive generalizations (G2/G3/O2/O3/C) that add registry-driven roles, declared rules, generation policies, and orchestration helpers, all while remaining additive.
apparent_themes: Additive evolution without breaking existing callers
Staged cognition and concurrent role waves
intention_signals: It is trying to turn hard-coded model-call orchestration into a declarative, addressable, rule-routed substrate where roles, skills, rules, and policies are data discovered from files, and concurrent outputs are resolved through a content-addressed store rather than in-memory returns.
novelty: The combination of file-discovered registries, run:// content-addressed store reads/writes, a declared AST rule evaluator for routing, a generation-policy ladder with finish_reason escalation, and explicit two-part staged cognition turns is unusual for a single runtime file.
contribution: A reusable runtime execution layer: registry loaders, the run_role runner with op/input axes, the run:// resolver, the staged-turn concurrency pattern, and the swarm/items/chunked/jury/panel/reduce/cascade orchestration primitives that a fused system could keep as the engine seam.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/cognition.py is a concurrent-cognition runtime proving spike that discovers roles, skills, contexts, guides, decisions, and generation policies from files, runs roles against a resident model, resolves run:// store references, and orchestrates staged turns, waves, juries, panels, reductions, and cascades.
intention_for_embedding: The file was meant to prove and then generalize a registry-driven, addressable, fail-loud concurrent-cognition execution substrate without breaking existing callers.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based role registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a RoleRegistry loaded from the roles/ directory so added or removed role files are picked up; shared by the cognition driver and the Suite.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based skill registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a SkillRegistry over skills/*.py; used when resolving skill:// addresses.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based guide registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a GuideRegistry over guides/*.py; described as the narrative how-to face of skills.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based context registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a ContextRegistry over contexts/*.py; used when resolving context:// addresses.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based decision registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a DecisionRegistry over decisions/*.py; decision:// frame/id resolves rows here, while state is composed separately from decision_take marks.

symbols.does: Maps a decision or subtype to a generation-policy id.
symbols.description: Looks up a decision record or bare subtype string in the decision_subtypes registry and returns the associated generation_policies id, or None when unknown so callers fall back to the default regime.

symbols.does: Discovers the file-based generation-policy registry fresh on each call.
symbols.description: Returns a GenerationPolicyRegistry over generation_policies/*.py; run_role(policy=id) reads the selected regime's repetition-penalty ladder from it.

symbols.does: Normalizes a role's declared input addresses.
symbols.description: Reads role.spec.input_addresses and returns a list; absent or empty means the default utterance-only input path.

symbols.does: Filters declared non-utterance inputs that are actually available this call.
symbols.description: Returns declared inputs other than utterance that are present in ctx or are addressable via a registered scheme when a store is supplied, keeping existing utterance-only callers on the byte-identical path.

symbols.does: Checks whether the call uses the default input axis.
symbols.description: True when no extra declared inputs are supplied, preserving the original f"Utterance: ..." framing.

symbols.does: Resolves one declared input by name.
symbols.description: Looks up bare names in ctx, resolves run:// addresses through resolve_run_ref, and resolves other registered schemes through resolve_address; raises on missing values or missing store.

symbols.does: Builds the raw text an embed role embeds.
symbols.description: Returns ctx["utterance"] for the default input path, otherwise concatenates supplied declared inputs as labelled lines.

symbols.does: Opt-in deliberate load for the embedder.
symbols.description: Delegates to ops/cli/capabilities.ensure_resident to make the configured embedder resident before embedding; may return a swap-needed approval dict instead of loading.

symbols.does: Fires a single role request at the resident model.
symbols.description: Supports generate and embed ops, resolves input and prompt/schema/thinking slots per coordinate, applies an optional generation-policy repetition-penalty ladder, and returns validated JSON or a vector dict; propagates FabricError on failure.

symbols.does: Persists corpus embeddings into projection spaces.
symbols.description: Groups records by embeddable projection and delegates each space to vector_index.build_index, using the existing space-keyed put_vector path; fail-loud on malformed records and non-embeddable projections.

symbols.does: Evaluates the declared recall-plus-ground routing rule.
symbols.description: Delegates to the rules engine over INJECTION_RULE_AST; injects recall.snippet when recall.relevant and ground.in_scope are both true, raising on missing declared inputs.

symbols.does: Dataclass capturing one staged-turn artifact.
symbols.description: Records turn id, utterance, focus output, emitted parts, timestamps, role finish order, routing decision, run:// addresses, and resolved role values.

symbols.does: Emits a two-part staged reply for an utterance.
symbols.description: Runs focus, yields Part 1 immediately, concurrently runs recall and ground writing to run:// addresses, joins the wave, reads addresses back, applies injection_rule, and yields enriched Part 2.

symbols.does: Worker thread for a wave role.
symbols.description: Runs one role, stores its validated output via put_content and set_ref, records finish order, and captures exceptions for fail-loud propagation after join.

symbols.does: Generator yielding the two parts.
symbols.description: Emits Part 1 before the wave completes, joins concurrent roles, resolves addresses, applies the injection rule, and yields Part 2.

symbols.does: Fires N concurrent role runs and reports latency.
symbols.description: Uses a ThreadPoolExecutor to run the same role N times against the resident pool, returning completion count and latency distribution.

symbols.does: Single probe invocation.
symbols.description: Runs one role call and records elapsed time; catches exceptions so the probe can report failures without aborting.

symbols.does: Percentile helper for probe latencies.
symbols.description: Computes a percentile from a sorted list of latencies for the probe report.

symbols.does: VRAM slot-budget dataclass.
symbols.description: Holds context-window budget parameters such as reserve and per-role context tokens; used to decide how many roles fit concurrently.

symbols.does: Builds a SlotBudget from services config.
symbols.description: Reads service configuration for a given service_id and constructs a SlotBudget with the specified reserves and token counts.

symbols.does: Returns a shared VramGate.
symbols.description: Creates or reuses a VramGate with the given limit to gate concurrent model execution by available VRAM.

symbols.does: Resolves a run:// address to its content.
symbols.description: Uses store.head to get the CAS address and store.get_content to read the value; raises or returns a sentinel based on on_missing.

symbols.does: Internal sentinel for bare names during address parsing.
symbols.description: Represents an unresolved bare name so the resolver can distinguish literal names from scheme addresses.

symbols.does: String representation of _BareName.
symbols.description: Returns a readable repr showing the wrapped name.

symbols.does: General address resolver for registered schemes.
symbols.description: Parses scheme and session/decision addresses, dispatches run:// via resolve_run_ref, and resolves other schemes through their registries; fail-loud on missing or unknown schemes.

symbols.does: Dataclass for a single role execution result.
symbols.description: Captures role id, output dict, elapsed time, finish reason, and optional error from one run_role invocation.

symbols.does: Dataclass for a concurrent wave outcome.
symbols.description: Aggregates a list of RoleRun records, total elapsed time, and any routing decision produced by the wave.

symbols.does: Runs a list of roles concurrently as a wave.
symbols.description: Submits each role to a thread pool, writes validated outputs to run:// addresses, reads them back, and returns a WaveResult.

symbols.does: Per-role swarm worker.
symbols.description: Executes one role in the swarm, persists its output to the store, and returns a RoleRun.

symbols.does: Dataclass for batched item execution.
symbols.description: Collects per-item RoleRun results and aggregate timing for run_items.

symbols.does: Runs a role over a list of addressed items.
symbols.description: Resolves each item, runs the role concurrently, writes each output to run://<turn>/items/<idx>, and returns an ItemsResult.

symbols.does: Exception wrapper for item resolution failures.
symbols.description: Wraps the original exception raised while resolving an item so the caller can distinguish resolution errors from role errors.

symbols.does: Initializes _ResolutionError.
symbols.description: Stores the causing exception for later inspection or propagation.

symbols.does: Resolves a single item value.
symbols.description: Handles bare values, run:// addresses, and other scheme addresses, returning the resolved content or raising _ResolutionError.

symbols.does: Per-item worker.
symbols.description: Resolves the item, runs the role, stores the output, and returns a RoleRun or None on resolution failure.

symbols.does: Reads a service's context window.
symbols.description: Loads services config and returns the configured context_window for the given service_id.

symbols.does: Computes safe text chunk size in characters.
symbols.description: Converts available context-window tokens into a character budget using configured output/prompt tokens, margin, and chars-per-token.

symbols.does: Splits text into fixed-size chunks.
symbols.description: Returns a list of chunks each up to chunk_chars length, preserving order.

symbols.does: Dataclass for chunked execution result.
symbols.description: Holds per-chunk outputs, the optional reduced result, and aggregate timing.

symbols.does: Runs a role over text chunks and optionally reduces.
symbols.description: Splits input text, runs the role per chunk, stores chunk outputs, optionally runs a reduce role or rule, and returns a ChunkedResult.

symbols.does: Dataclass for jury outcome.
symbols.description: Collects multiple draws, their values, and a verdict tally from run_jury.

symbols.does: Runs multiple temperature-1 draws of a role and tallies.
symbols.description: Executes N varied draws concurrently, stores each at run://<turn>/jury/<i>, and returns a JuryResult with verdict distribution.

symbols.does: Single jury draw worker.
symbols.description: Runs one draw with elevated temperature and returns its RoleRun.

symbols.does: Runs a declared panel of roles and resolves their outputs.
symbols.description: Takes a panel dict, resolves each member role, runs them, reads back addresses, and returns aggregated panel results.

symbols.does: Helper to read a set of run:// addresses.
symbols.description: Iterates over addresses, resolves each via head and get_content, and returns tuples of role id, address, and value; honors on_missing.

symbols.does: Dataclass for reduce outcome.
symbols.description: Holds the reduced value, source addresses, cluster assignments, and timing for run_reduce.

symbols.does: Tallies a list of values.
symbols.description: Counts occurrences and returns the most common value plus frequency distribution.

symbols.does: Factory for field-based tally rules.
symbols.description: Returns a reduce rule function that tallies values by a specified field.

symbols.does: Generated tally rule.
symbols.description: Applies _verdict_tally to the list of values extracted by the configured field.

symbols.does: Resolves a reduce rule by name.
symbols.description: Maps built-in rule names like majority or first to their tally or selection functions.

symbols.does: Reduces a set of addressed role outputs.
symbols.description: Reads back addresses, optionally embeds and clusters them, then applies a reduce rule or role to produce a ReduceResult.

symbols.does: Extracts embeddable text from a reduce value.
symbols.description: Serializes a value to a stable string suitable for embedding during clustering.

symbols.does: Clusters reduce units by embedding similarity.
symbols.description: Embeds each read-back value and groups them by cosine-similarity threshold into clusters.

symbols.does: Classifies a cascade step.
symbols.description: Inspects a step dict and returns its kind string, used by run_cascade to dispatch to swarm, items, jury, panel, reduce, or other primitives.

symbols.does: Executes a declarative cascade action.
symbols.description: Interprets a cascade action dict, resolves roles and inputs, and dispatches to the appropriate cognition primitive to produce a result.
